title: calophyllum touranense
intro: Calophyllum touranense, also known as the Vietnamese calophyllum, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the family Clusiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Calophyllum touranense is believed to offer various health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support wound healing, as well as exhibit anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application.
